09:30 ‚ÄĒ departure from BakńĪ to Gusar with a stop near Beshbarmag Mountain. On the way you will see the arched bridge across the Gudiyalchay river and the Red Village in Quba district, home to one of the largest communities of Mountain Jews in the world.

Itinerary: BakńĪ ‚Äď Gusar ‚Äď Ňěahdańü ‚Äď Laza ‚Äď BakńĪ
Gusar is the last major settlement in the north of Az…ôrbaycan, called ‚ÄúThe North Gates‚ÄĚ as it borders the Republic of Dagestan. The population consists of Lezgins ‚ÄĒ an original nationality with a rich culture known for hospitality. Four of the nine climatic zones are found in Gusar. The region is famous for the Ňěahdańü summer‚Äďwinter complex, operating all year round: a popular ski resort in winter and a camp site on the slopes of the Greater Qafqaz in summer. Part of the territory belongs to Ňěahdańü National Park ‚ÄĒ the largest in Az…ôrbaycan. Historical monuments include the fortress walls in Enih village (13th century), the 1544 tomb of Sheikh Juneyd, and the 18th-century mosques of Kohne Khudat and Khuray. The folklore ensemble ‚ÄúLezginka‚ÄĚ keeps alive the famous Caucasian dance of the same name.
Laza village lies on the north-east outskirts of the Main Caucasian Ridge, at the foot of the ‚ÄúShah Yaylag‚ÄĚ plateau and the 4,242 m Ňěahdańü peak, at an altitude of 1,300 m on an ancient caravan route. Its population consists mostly of ethnic Lezgins whose history is closely connected with Caucasian Albania, one of the most ancient states on the territory of Az…ôrbaycan. In winter most of the waterfalls freeze and climbing competitions are organized on the frozen falls.

tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.10.sections.0: Understanding the development of medieval Icheri Sheher and urban life in BakńĪ is key to understanding the current economic, material, and moral culture of present-day Az…ôrbaycan.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.10.sections.1:
Maiden Tower ‚ÄĒ the symbol of BakńĪ in the southeastern part of Icheri Sheher.
Juma Mosque (12th century) ‚ÄĒ a center of the social, political, and cultural life of medieval Az…ôrbaycan.
Old caravanserais ‚ÄĒ the 14th-century Multani Caravanserai on Qulle Street and the Bukhara Caravanserai opposite, shelters for merchants on the caravan routes.
Bathhouses ‚ÄĒ half-underground hammams, warm in winter and cool in summer.
Khanegahs ‚ÄĒ dwellings of the Sufis along the trade routes.
Shirvanshah‚Äôs Palace (12th‚Äď15th century) ‚ÄĒ the last residence of the rulers of Shirvan, built after the capital moved to BakńĪ following the 1191 earthquake.
Museum of Miniature Books ‚ÄĒ 4,350 miniature books from 62 countries on display.
The Mugham Theatre ‚ÄĒ a 15th-century two-floored caravanserai with an octagonal courtyard.
Fountain Square, the National History Museum, a sea trip along BakńĪ Bay, and BakńĪ‚Äôs largest shopping malls.

tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.11.sections.0: Spend a day like a real BakńĪ citizen and try the best dishes in authentic restaurants. This excursion opens the world of the richest Azerbaijani cuisine: a gastronomic journey through the Old City, the colorful BakńĪ bazaar, and the coast of the Caspian Sea. In one day you will learn new shades of flavors and try local delicacies ‚ÄĒ the most delicious lamb and sturgeon, fragrant wines, countless spices, and dried fruits.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.11.sections.1:
S…ôh…ôr yem…ôyi in the Old Town ‚ÄĒ rustic butter, cheese, honey, hot cakes freshly baked in tandir, tea or coffee.
The eastern bazaar ‚ÄĒ one of the oldest BakńĪ bazaars: the specifics of Azerbaijani cuisine, its main ingredients and spices; taste and buy spices, dried fruits, fresh Caspian black caviar, and exclusive Azerbaijani teas.
Lunch ‚ÄĒ shish kebab from freshly caught sturgeon or lamb, shah-plov, and other traditional dishes at a traditional restaurant.
Authentic dishes and secret recipes ‚ÄĒ a stroll through the iconic sights of the Old Town with tea or wine tasting.
Organizational details ‚ÄĒ food and drinks are paid separately; transportation is included. Tell us your culinary preferences in advance.

tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.12.sections.0: Gabala is a unique and beautiful region on the bank of the Demiraparanchay River, overlooking the highest peak of Az…ôrbaycan ‚ÄĒ Bazardyuzyu (4,466 m). The main sight is the ruins of an ancient town 15 km from the city center: ‚ÄúKabalaka,‚ÄĚ mentioned in 1st-century historical sources and for 600 years the capital of Caucasian Albania.

In the 9th‚Äď16th centuries it was the capital of the Shirvan khanate, residence of the Shirvanshahs, and one of the most beautiful cities of the East. tours.byCountry.poland.2.excerpt: UNESCO underground world: salt chapels, brine lakes, and 135 m descents.
tours.byCountry.poland.2.meta.0.label: Sites
tours.byCountry.poland.2.meta.1.label: Tur length
tours.byCountry.poland.2.sections.0.body: The Wieliczka Salt Mine was one of the first 12 sites in the world designated a UNESCO World Heritage Site and is one of the most visited tourist sites anywhere. The 2-hour tour covers only 1% of the underground world: winding corridors almost 3 km long, 800 steps, and a descent to a depth of 135 meters through the Danilowicz Shaft. Explore a vast labyrinth of tunnels and chambers and admire intricate statues, chandeliers, and other art carved directly in the salt rock.
tours.byCountry.poland.2.sections.0.head: Wieliczka Salt Mine
tours.byCountry.poland.2.sections.1.body: Hear the story of Duke Boleslaw V the Chaste and Duchess Kinga, ride the underground railway, go down the world‚Äôs longest underground slide, and sail a ferry on the brine lake flooding the chamber. Visit a network of chambers with unusual geological structures, a stable, an exhibition of wooden tread-wheels, and the Chapel of Saint Kinga at a depth of 212 meters underground.
